name: Atlantic Way
name_original: Praia de Santa Cruz — percurso circular
description: Jog this moderate 5.4-mile circular route along the Praia de Santa Cruz coastline, featuring stunning ocean views and the iconic Penedo do G
region_intro: Jogging routes around Silveira, Portugal, traverse a diverse landscape characterized by riverine and coastal environments. The region features extensive trail networks that follow riverbanks, lead to beaches, and wind through rural areas. Runners can experience varied terrain, including valleys with alluvial deposits, sloped cliffs, and expansive sandy beaches. Dedicated ecotrails, such as the Sizandro Ecotrail, offer paths through natural areas with views of rivers and agricultural fields. Best jogging routes around Silveira The most popular jogging route is Atlantic Way, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 1 minute to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Atlantic coast. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from Aeroclube de Torres Vedras, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.6 km) path. This route explores rural landscapes and varied terrain around the Aeroclube. Local runners also love the Praia Azul viewpoint loop from Aeroclube de Torres Vedras, an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail leading through coastal scenery and offering viewpoints, often completed in about 1 hour 25 minutes. Jogging around Silveira is defined by coastal paths, riverine trails, and rural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes. The routes in Silveira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from a handful of reviews. More than 400 runners have used komoot to explore Silveira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Silveira?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes around Silveira listed on komoot, offering a variety of distances and terrains for joggers. These include coastal paths, riverine trails, and routes through rural landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Silveira?
A: While most routes are classified as moderate, the region offers paths that can be enjoyed by beginners. For instance, sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381187853/atlantic-way" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atlantic Way&lt;/a&gt; are relatively flat and offer scenic coastal views, making them a good starting point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4211053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sizandro Ecotrail&lt;/a&gt; also provides a gentle experience through natural areas.

Q: What are some good options for long-distance running in Silveira?
A: For longer runs, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21932033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio Alcabrichel – Praia de Santa Helena loop from Aeroclube de Torres Vedras&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 17 km. Another excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21930676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia Azul viewpoint loop from Aeroclube de Torres Vedras&lt;/a&gt;, offering a challenging 13 km run with coastal scenery.

Q: Are there any circular jogging routes in the Silveira area?
A: Yes, many of the running paths in Silveira are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21930715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Aeroclube de Torres Vedras&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route covering 10.6 km through rural landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6899974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz do Sizandro loop from Aeroclube de Torres Vedras&lt;/a&gt; is another great circular option.

Q: What scenic attractions can I see while running in Silveira?
A: Silveira's routes offer diverse scenery. You can run along the Alcabrichel River, explore picturesque beaches like Porto Novo Beach, or enjoy panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3538591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto da Vela Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/628971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penedo do Guincho&lt;/a&gt; at Santa Cruz Beach is also a notable landmark offering stunning coastal vistas.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly running trails in Silveira?
A: Many of the natural trails and rural paths around Silveira are suitable for running with dogs, provided they are kept on a leash and you adhere to local regulations. The extensive trail networks along riverbanks and through rural landscapes offer plenty of space for you and your canine companion to enjoy.

Q: Are there family-friendly running options in Silveira?
A: For families, the flatter sections of coastal paths or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4211053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sizandro Ecotrail&lt;/a&gt; are excellent choices. These routes often feature wooden walkways and offer a gentler experience, allowing families to enjoy the natural beauty together at a comfortable pace.

Q: Are there running routes in Silveira with cafes or rest areas nearby?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4211053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sizandro Ecotrail&lt;/a&gt; is known for connecting various towns and villages, and it includes amenities such as rest areas and cafes along its path, making it convenient for a refreshing break during your run.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Silveira?
A: The running routes in Silveira are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from coastal paths with stunning Atlantic views to tranquil riverine trails and rural landscapes, offering a varied and engaging experience for all abilities.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Silveira?
A: Silveira offers pleasant jogging conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures, ideal for longer runs. Even in winter, the climate is generally temperate, making it possible to enjoy the trails, though some coastal paths might be windier. Summer mornings and evenings are best to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any running routes accessible by public transport in Silveira?
A: The region around Torres Vedras, including Silveira, has local bus services that connect towns and villages. While specific route access points by public transport vary, many trails, especially those connecting urban centers or popular beaches like Porto Novo, are often within reasonable walking distance from bus stops. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for specific starting points.
tips: Santa Cruz, with its extensive sand divided into several beaches, is famous for the purity of its waters and sand and the beauty of its picturesque rocks. Penedo do Guincho stands out, an impressive 30 meter high rock. From the top of the terraces, it is possible to admire a vast panorama, including the Cabo Carvoeiro lighthouse and the Berlengas Islands. The region is well equipped with infrastructure, including a swimming pool and even an Olympic shooting pit.
At the north end of Santa Cruz is one of the calmest spots on the beach, right in front of the cliff. Large rocks on the sand.
The trail over the cliff offers a complete panorama of Santa Cruz beach. Very quiet place, taking into account that it is located close to Lisbon.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Praia de Santa Cruz circular route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking available near the main beach areas of Santa Cruz. These spots offer convenient access to the start of the circular route, allowing you to begin your jog right by the coast.

faqs.question: Is the Praia de Santa Cruz circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is generally accessible, specific regulations for dogs on the beaches and boardwalks in Santa Cruz may vary by season or local ordinance. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash and be mindful of other users, especially in crowded areas. Please check local signage for any restrictions.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog the Praia de Santa Cruz circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is enjoyable year-round, but the shoulder seasons (spring and early autumn) often offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and fewer crowds. Summer can be warm, making early mornings or late afternoons ideal for jogging. Winter can be cooler and windier, but still offers stunning coastal views.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route or the Rede Natura do Oeste?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or to pay an entrance fee to jog the Praia de Santa Cruz circular route. The trail is freely accessible to the public. As it passes through sections of the Rede Natura do Oeste, general regulations for protected natural areas apply, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing wildlife, but there are no specific fees for entry.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Praia de Santa Cruz circular route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly paved surfaces, making it suitable for jogging. However, the Santa Cruz area also offers diverse natural terrain, including long sandy beaches, dramatic cliffs, and picturesque rock formations. You'll also find a pleasant seafront boardwalk along parts of the route.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to stunning Atlantic coastline views. Key highlights include the impressive Penedo do Guincho, Santa Cruz Beach, a 30-meter-high rock formation with an arch. You'll also pass by the dramatic Cliffs and Rock Formations of Santa Cruz Beach and the beautiful Santa Cruz Beach and Waterfall. Viewpoints along the way offer panoramic vistas, potentially including the Cabo Carvoeiro lighthouse and the Berlengas Islands in the distance on clear days.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Praia de Santa Cruz circular route intersects with several other official trails. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Caminho do Atlântico - Rede Natura do Oeste and the Rota do Atlântico. It also briefly touches parts of the EuroVelo 1 - Atlantic Coast Route.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 8.7 kilometers (5.4 miles) long. For an average jogger, it typically takes around 1 hour to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, Santa Cruz maintains an authentic, laid-back atmosphere with various facilities. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and other amenities in the town center and along the beachfront, perfect for a refreshment before or after your jog.

faqs.question: Is the Praia de Santa Cruz circular route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Described as 'moderate' in difficulty, this route is suitable for joggers with good basic fitness. While it features mostly paved surfaces and a relatively low elevation gain, its length of 8.7 km means beginners might find it a good challenge to work towards, or they can choose to complete shorter sections.

faqs.question: What makes this route unique for jogging?
faqs.answer: This route stands out for its unique combination of natural beauty and accessibility. It offers stunning Atlantic coastline views, diverse scenery including sandy beaches and dramatic cliffs, and the iconic Penedo do Guincho. The presence of a seafront boardwalk provides an enjoyable coastal running experience, blending nature with a comfortable path.
